---
title: "GESECA"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-13"
---

```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(fgsea)
library(Seurat)
library(Matrix)

liver_data = readRDS("C:/Dev/Code/xGATE/Paper_code/human_liver.rds")
head(liver_data@meta.data)
metadata = liver_data@meta.data

hepatocyte_cells = metadata[metadata$cell_type == "hepatocyte",]

count_matrix <- liver_data@assays[["RNA"]]@counts

liver_count_matrix = count_matrix[, rownames(hepatocyte_cells)]


# --- 1. PREPARE THE DATA ---

# A. Normalize the Count Matrix
# GESECA expects an expression matrix (usually log-normalized), not raw counts.
# We use Seurat's standard normalization (Log1p)
print("Normalizing count matrix...")
# We use Seurat's internal function to normalize the sparse matrix efficiently
norm_matrix <- NormalizeData(liver_count_matrix, verbose = FALSE)

# B. Convert to Dense Matrix (Required for GESECA)
# Note: If you have >50k cells, this might use a lot of RAM. 
# Since we are subsetting hepatocytes, it should be fine.
E_matrix <- as.matrix(norm_matrix)

# --- 2. ID COMPATIBILITY CHECK ---

library(pagoda2)
library(jsonlite)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(AnnotationDbi)
library(Matrix)
library(Seurat)



# Define the specific pathways to test
target_pathways <- c(
  "Carbon metabolism", "Biosynthesis of amino acids", "Biosynthesis of cofactors",
  "Glycolysis / Gluconeogenesis", "Pentose and glucuronate interconversions",
  "Ascorbate and aldarate metabolism", "Pyruvate metabolism", "Fatty acid degradation",
  "Primary bile acid biosynthesis", "Steroid hormone biosynthesis",
  "Arachidonic acid metabolism", "Linoleic acid metabolism",
  "Biosynthesis of unsaturated fatty acids", "Glycine, serine and threonine metabolism",
  "Cysteine and methionine metabolism", "Tyrosine metabolism",
  "Taurine and hypotaurine metabolism", "Retinol metabolism",
  "Porphyrin metabolism", "Metabolism of xenobiotics by cytochrome P450",
  "Cholesterol metabolism", "Caffeine metabolism", "Drug metabolism", "Thyroid cancer",
  "Shigellosis", "Colorectal cancer", "Pancreatic cancer", "Hepatocellular carcinoma",
  "Gastric cancer", "Glioma", "Acute myeloid leukemia", "Chronic myeloid leukemia",
  "Basal cell carcinoma", "Melanoma", "Renal cell carcinoma", "Bladder cancer",
  "Prostate cancer", "Endometrial cancer", "Breast cancer",
  "Small cell lung cancer", "Non-small cell lung cancer"
)

# --- Prepare the Pathway Environment ---

# Load the Master JSON file
json_data <- fromJSON("C:/Dev/Code/xGATE/Paper_code/R_code/pathway_genes_master_list.json")

# Filter JSON to keep only the requested pathways
valid_targets <- intersect(names(json_data), target_pathways)
filtered_list <- json_data[valid_targets]
print(paste("Processing", length(valid_targets), "pathways..."))

# Helper Function: Convert IDs and Clean Attributes
clean_and_convert <- function(kegg_ids) {
  # 1. Remove "hsa:" prefix
  entrez_ids <- gsub("hsa:", "", kegg_ids)
  
  # 2. Map Entrez to Ensembl
  ensembl_ids <- mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db, 
                        keys = entrez_ids, 
                        column = "ENSEMBL", 
                        keytype = "ENTREZID", 
                        multiVals = "first")
  
  # 3. Remove NAs
  clean_ids <- na.omit(ensembl_ids)
  
  # 4. CRITICAL: Strip all attributes (names, na.action) to prevent 'non-function' error
  clean_ids <- as.vector(clean_ids)
  clean_ids <- unname(clean_ids)
  attributes(clean_ids) <- NULL 
  
  return(clean_ids)
}

# Apply conversion and cleaning
final_list <- lapply(filtered_list, clean_and_convert)

# Remove any empty pathways
final_list <- final_list[sapply(final_list, length) > 0]

# 'final_list' uses Ensembl IDs (from your cleaning function).
matrix_genes <- rownames(E_matrix)
pathway_genes <- unique(unlist(final_list))

overlap_count <- length(intersect(matrix_genes, pathway_genes))
print(paste("Total overlapping genes between Matrix and Pathways:", overlap_count))


# --- 3. RUN GESECA ---

print("Running GESECA...")
set.seed(42)

# Run the analysis
# minSize=5 ensures we keep smaller pathways
geseca_results <- geseca(
  pathways = final_list,
  E = E_matrix,
  minSize = 5,
  maxSize = 2000,
  center = TRUE # Performs centering as per the vignette
)

# --- 4. FORMAT & SAVE RESULTS ---

# Convert to Dataframe
results_df <- as.data.frame(geseca_results)
print(results_df)

# Create the Output structure
csv_output <- data.frame(
  Pathway.Name = results_df$pathway,
  Type = "GESECA",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Create Binary Value based on Significance
# If Adjusted P-value < 0.05 -> 1 (Co-regulated/Active)
# Otherwise -> -1 (Inactive)
csv_output$Value <- ifelse(results_df$padj < 0.05, 1, -1)

# Sort by P-value (most significant first)
# We use the original results_df to sort, then apply order to csv_output
csv_output <- csv_output[order(results_df$padj), ]

# --- 5. SAVE TO CSV ---
#write.csv(csv_output, "hepatocyte_geseca_pathways.csv", row.names = FALSE)



```

```{r}
library(fgsea)
library(Seurat)
library(Matrix)
library(jsonlite)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(AnnotationDbi)

# --- 1. SETUP PATHWAYS & DATA (Your Code) ---

# Define target pathways
target_pathways <- c(
  "Autophagy", "Protein processing in endoplasmic reticulum", "mTOR signaling pathway",
  "MAPK signaling pathway", "Apoptosis", "Longevity regulating pathway",
  "HIF-1 signaling pathway", "AMPK signaling pathway", "Phagosome",
  "PPAR signaling pathway", "FoxO signaling pathway", "cGMP-PKG signaling pathway",
  "Cytokine-cytokine receptor interaction", "Mineral absorption",
  "Antigen processing and presentation", "Rap1 signaling pathway", "Ribosome",
  "Protein digestion and absorption", "PI3K-Akt signaling pathway",
  "Insulin signaling pathway", "Oxidative phosphorylation", "Pancreatic secretion",
  "Salmonella infection", "Bacterial invasion of epithelial cells",
  "Pathogenic Escherichia coli infection", "Thyroid cancer", "Shigellosis"
)

# Load and Clean JSON Pathways
json_data <- fromJSON("C:/Dev/Code/xGATE/Paper_code/R_code/pathway_genes_master_list.json")
valid_targets <- intersect(names(json_data), target_pathways)
filtered_list <- json_data[valid_targets]

# Helper to clean IDs
clean_and_convert <- function(kegg_ids) {
  entrez_ids <- gsub("hsa:", "", kegg_ids)
  ensembl_ids <- mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db, keys = entrez_ids, column = "ENSEMBL", keytype = "ENTREZID", multiVals = "first")
  clean_ids <- na.omit(ensembl_ids)
  clean_ids <- as.vector(clean_ids) # Strip attributes
  clean_ids <- unname(clean_ids)
  attributes(clean_ids) <- NULL 
  return(clean_ids)
}

final_list <- lapply(filtered_list, clean_and_convert)
final_list <- final_list[sapply(final_list, length) > 0]

# Load Data
pancreas_data <- readRDS("C:/Dev/Code/xGATE/Paper_code/pancreas_human.rds")
metadata <- pancreas_data@meta.data

# Subset Control Beta Cells
cells_ctrl <- rownames(metadata[metadata$cell_type == "type B pancreatic cell" & 
                                metadata$disease_state == "Control",])
count_matrix_ctrl <- pancreas_data@assays[["RNA"]]@counts[, cells_ctrl]

# Prepare Matrix
clean_counts_ctrl <- as(as.matrix(count_matrix_ctrl), "dgCMatrix")

# Strip version numbers from gene IDs if present (to match your JSON)
if(any(grepl("\\.[0-9]+$", rownames(clean_counts_ctrl)))) {
  rownames(clean_counts_ctrl) <- sub("\\.[0-9]+$", "", rownames(clean_counts_ctrl))
}

# --- 2. PREPARE FOR GESECA ---

# A. Normalize the Count Matrix
print("Normalizing count matrix...")
# Log1p Normalization
norm_matrix <- NormalizeData(clean_counts_ctrl, verbose = FALSE)

# B. Convert to Dense Matrix
# Since you have subsetted to specific cells, this should fit in memory.
E_matrix <- as.matrix(norm_matrix)

# C. Overlap Check
matrix_genes <- rownames(E_matrix)
pathway_genes <- unique(unlist(final_list))
overlap_count <- length(intersect(matrix_genes, pathway_genes))
print(paste("Total overlapping genes:", overlap_count))

if(overlap_count < 5) {
  stop("ERROR: Low gene overlap. Check if your Pancreas RDS uses Ensembl IDs or Symbols.")
}

# --- 3. RUN GESECA ---

print("Running GESECA...")
set.seed(42)

# Run Analysis
geseca_results <- geseca(
  pathways = final_list,
  E = E_matrix,
  minSize = 5,
  maxSize = 2000,
  center = TRUE
)

# --- 4. FORMAT & SAVE ---

results_df <- as.data.frame(geseca_results)
print(results_df)

# Create Output Structure (Matching your Pagoda File)
csv_output <- data.frame(
  Pathway.Name = results_df$pathway,
  Type = "GESECA",
  Group = "Ctrl",  # Added Group column
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Create Binary Value
# Logic: Adjusted P-value < 0.05 = Active (1), else -1
csv_output$Value <- ifelse(results_df$padj < 0.05, 1, -1)

# Sort by Significance (P-value)
csv_output <- csv_output[order(results_df$padj), ]

# Save to CSV
#write.csv(csv_output, "pancreas_geseca_pathways_ctrl.csv", row.names = FALSE)





```

```{r}
sessionInfo()

```
